hypothesis: A continuation-based refinement that optimizes against a differentiable XRD simulator with initially large pseudo-Voigt peak widths (e.g., Gaussian/Lorentzian FWHM ≳1.0°) that are exponentially annealed to instrument-accurate Caglioti parameters will reduce spurious local minima and, without symmetry constraints, increase StructureMatcher match rates by at least 20 percentage points over fixed-width optimization at σl=0.1 and σc=0.05.
